entries.definition:
كتاب الميم

ملو

كَغِرقئِ بيض كنَّه القيضُ من عَلِ

فملّكَ باللِّيط الذي فوق قِشرها

والأصل هذا. ثم قيلَ مَلَكَ الإنسانُ الشَّيء يملِكُه مَلْكاً. والاسم الملْك؛ لأنَّ يدَه فيه قويّةٌ صحيحة. فالمِلْك: ما مُلِك من مالٍ. والمملوك: العبْد. وفلانٌ حَسن المَلَكة، أي حسن الصَّنيع إلى ممالكيه. وعبدُ مَمْلكةٍ: سُبِيَ ولم يُملَك أبواه. وما لفلانٍ مولى مَلاَكةٍ دونَ الله تعالى، أي لم يملكْه إلاّ هو. وَكنّا [في] إملاكِ فلانٍ، أي أملكناه امرأتَه. وأملكناه مثل ملّكناه. والمَلَك: الماء يكون مع المسافر، لأنَّه إذا كان معه مَلَك أمرَه.

(ملو) الميم واللام والحرف المعتلّ أصل صحيح يدلُّ على امتدادٍ في شيءٍ زمانٍ أو غيره. وأملَيت القيدَ للبعير إملاءً، إذا وسَّعته. وتملّيت عُمْرِي، إذا استمتَعت به. والمَلَوانِ: اللّيل والنهار. والملاوة : ملاوة العيش، أي قد أُمليَ له. ومن الباب إملاء الكتاب.

والله أعلم بالصَّواب.

(باب ماجاء من كلام العرب على أكثر من ثلاثة أحرف أوله ميم)

................

ـــــــــ

تم كتاب الميم والله أعلم بالصواب

نه نأ نب نث

م/ل/و m–l–w an expanse of empty land; a period of time, to prolong
life for someone, prosperity; to give rein to; to dictate (a text). Of
this root, three forms occur six times in the Qur’an: أَمْلَى ʾamlā four
times; تُمْلَى tumlā once and مَلِيًّا maliyyan once.

أَمْلَى ʾamlā [v. IV, intrans. with prep. لـ] to give rein (to), to
grant respite (to) (3:178) وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ
لِأَنْفُسِهِمْ إِنَّمَا نُمْلِي لَهُمْ لِيَزْدَادُوا إِثْمًا and those who disbelieve should not
imagine that We give them free rein for their own good; We give
them rein so that they become more sinful.

تُمْلَى tumlā [imperf. pass. of v. IV, trans.] (of a text) to be
dictated (25:5) فَهِيَ تُمْلَى عَلَيْهِ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا so they are continuously
dictated to him [lit. morning and evening].

مَلِيًّا maliyyan [adverbial] a considerably long time (19:46)
وَاهْجُرْنِي مَلِيًّا keep out of my way for good [lit. for a long while]!

مَن man nominal occurring 861 times in the Qur’an with one of the
following functions: 1 relative pronoun for rational beings
(اسم موصول للعاقل): ‘who’, ‘the one who’, ‘those who’, ‘whoever’,
‘whosoever’ (9:27) ثُمَّ يَتُوبُ اللَّهُ مِنْ بَعْدِ ذَلِكَ عَلَى مَنْ يَشَاءُ but God turns
in His mercy to whomever He will. Occasionally, man refers to
non-rational beings or a mixture of rational and non-rational
beings (24:45) وَاللَّهُ خَلَقَ كُلَّ دَابَّةٍ مِنْ مَاءٍ فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَمْشِي عَلَى بَطْنِهِ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ
يَمْشِي عَلَى رِجْلَيْنِ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَمْشِي عَلَى أَرْبَعٍ God created each living
creature out of [a type of] water: some of them crawl on their
bellies, some walk on two legs, and some on four 2 interrogative
pronoun for rational beings (اسم استفهام للعاقل): ‘who?’, ‘which
one?’, ‘which ones?’ (7:32) مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي أَخْرَجَ لِعِبَادِهِ وَالطَّيِّبَاتِ مِنَ
الرِّزْقِ who has forbidden the beautiful things which God has
brought forth for His servants and that which is wholesome from
the sustenance [He has provided]?! 3 conditional pronoun for
rational beings (اسم شرط للعاقل): ‘whosoever’ (4:123) مَنْ يَعْمَلْ
سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ whosoever does wrong will be requited for it.

مِن min preposition (حرف جرّ) occurring 3221 times in the Qur’an and
indicating certain contextually determined meanings: 1 departure,
starting point (الابتداء) a) from a place (17:1) مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ

897

من

الْأَقْصَى from the Sacred Mosque to the Furthest Mosque b) from a
point in time (9:108) لَمَسْجِدٌ أُسِّسَ عَلَى التَّقْوَى مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ [indeed] a
mosque that is founded on consciousness of God from the first
day … c) from a person (27:30) إِنَّهُ مِنْ سُلَيْمَانَ it is from Solomon d)
from a state or condition (2:257) اللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُخْرِجُهُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى
النُّورِ God is the ally of those who believe–He brings them out of
darkness into light 2 away from (المجاوزة) (4:75) الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا
أَخْرِجْنَا مِنْ هَذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ الظَّالِمِ أَهْلُهَا those who say, ‘Our Lord, take us
away from [deliver us from] this town whose people are
oppressors’ 3 ‘some of’ (التبعيض) (2:253) مِنْهُمْ مَنْ كَلَّمَ اللَّهُ to some of
them God spoke 4 ‘type of’ (بيان الجنس) (35:2) مَا يَفْتَحِ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ
رَحْمَةٍ whatever of mercy God grants to people, there is none
to withhold it 5 reason or cause of an action (التعليل), ‘because of’
(71:25) مِمَّا خَطِيئَاتِهِمْ أُغْرِقُوا فَأُدْخِلُوا نَارًا because of their sins they were
drowned and cast into a Fire 6 ‘instead of’ (البدل) (43:60) وَلَوْ نَشَاءُ
لَجَعَلْنَا مِنْكُمْ مَلَائِكَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ يَخْلُفُونَ if We had willed so, We could have
made instead of you angels (or, made some of you into angels) to
inherit [the earth] 7 sense of prep. ʿan (معنى عن) of, off (21:97)
يَاوَيْلَنَا قَدْ كُنَّا فِي غَفْلَةٍ مِنْ هَذَا woe to us!, we were heedless of this 8
sense of prep. bi (معنى بـ) ‘with’ (42:45) يَنْظُرُونَ مِنْ طَرْفٍ خَفِيٍّ they
look, glancing furtively [lit. with a hidden glance] 9 sense of prep.
fī (معنى في) ‘in’, ‘within’ (62:9) إِذَا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلَاةِ مِنْ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ when the
call to prayer is made on/within the day of congregation 10 sense
of prep. ʿalā (معنى على) ‘over’, ‘above’ (21:77) وَنَصَرْنَاهُ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ
كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا We helped him gain victory over (or, We took him away
from) the people who denied Our signs 11 sense of adverb
ʿind (معنى عند) (58:17) لَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَالُهُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُهُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا neither
their wealth nor their children will be of any use to them
with/against God 12 sense of ‘as distinguished from’ (المخالفة)
(2:220) وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْمُفْسِدَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِ and God knows the corruptors
from the reformers 13 ‘other than’ (المغايرة) (21:42) قُلْ مَنْ
يَكْلَؤُكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ مِنَ الرَّحْمَنِ say, ‘Who could shelter you night and day other
than [lit. away from] the Lord of Mercy?’ 14 the so-called
‘redundant’ min (من الزائدة) used for emphasis, [‘not] any one’, in
conjunction with a) a negative (نفي) (6:59) وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِنْ وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا
not a leaf falls, but He knows it or b) a rhetorical question

898
